commands.description: Execute arbitrary SQL queries against HyperFleet database

args: SELECT id, name, generation FROM clusters LIMIT 5
SELECT id, name, generation FROM node_pools
SELECT table_name FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'
executions.output:
table_name    
------------------
 migrations
 adapter_statuses
 clusters
 node_pools
(4 rows)

executions.notes: Shows full adapter_statuses row including JSON conditions column

commands.description: Delete rows from a specific table, with confirmation preview
behavior: Shows count of records to be deleted
Can delete by specific ID or all rows in table
commands.notes: Interactive - requires user confirmation, not recorded

commands.description: Delete ALL records from adapter_statuses, node_pools, and clusters tables
behavior: Deletes in order: adapter_statuses → node_pools → clusters (respecting FK constraints)
commands.notes: Interactive - requires user confirmation, not recorded

commands.description: Query adapter_statuses from PostgreSQL running in Kubernetes
behavior: Finds postgres pod in k8s cluster
Runs SQL query against it via kubectl exec
commands.notes: Different from hf.db.query.sh - this connects through kubectl to in-cluster postgres

commands.description: Delete adapter statuses from in-cluster PostgreSQL
behavior: Deletes adapter_statuses scoped to current cluster-id

commands.description: Interactive configuration of PostgreSQL connection parameters
behavior: Prompts for host, port, name, user, password
commands.notes: Interactive - prompts for input, not recorded
